domain: Toraja tongkonan ancestor house (Sulawesi Indonesia; stilt-pile elevated 3-tier saddleback structure; ground/middle/upper levels; relics+heirlooms at highest tier; N-S cosmological orientation; lineage anchor of founding ancestor)
specific_mechanism: Three-tier attention mask with ASYMMETRIC ELEVATION RULE: tokens are classified into tiers — tier-3 (sacred/priority: system prompt + safety + retrieval-grounded), tier-2 (active context: recent N tokens), tier-1 (background: older context). Cross-tier attention follows: tier-3 → ALL (priority broadcast); tier-2 → tier-2 + tier-1 (no upward access except via embedded tier-3 anchors); tier-1 → tier-1 only.
llm_application: TONGKONAN-TIER-GATE: a 3-tier elevation-asymmetric attention mask. (1) Classify tokens at insertion into 3 tiers based on role (sacred/system/safety = tier-3, active context recent = tier-2, background older = tier-1); (2) attention mask matrix M ∈ {0,1}^{L×L} constructed per the asymmetric rule M[i,j] = 1 if tier(j) ≥ tier(i) ELSE asymmetric exception (cannot upward attend). (3) tier-3 tokens broadcast everywhere; tier-1 tokens see only themselves. Optional decay: re-classify token tier from tier-2 to tier-1 after N tokens (eviction analog of elevation drop). Differs from R401 AINU-INAU instruction-prompt-tier-stack (priority encoded via repetition count, not elevation-asymmetric attention mask), Qwen3-Next gated attention (sigmoid-gate per head, not tier asymmetric mask), R412 MAJLIS dual-axis position+status (status-inequality, not 3-tier-asymmetric).
novelty_claim: Sliding-window + system-prompt priority + sparse-attention masks cover attention-pattern space. TONGKONAN-TIER-GATE adds explicit 3-tier ASYMMETRIC ELEVATION rule + tier-decay-on-age. Recombination of priority-attention + sliding-window + system-prompt isolation.
